title: Skill binding entry (v2 — Facet B)
description: A single role skill binding entry as it appears in charter tooling.<role>.skills[] (plan §4.1 Facet B + §5). Matches the catalog shape in skills/registry.yaml. HARD RULES (Constitution §1.7 + §3.4 invariant #6, process/role-skill-model.md §4): vendored + pinned, NO runtime fetch; the skill's tool_requirements MUST be a subset of the role tool whitelist; redistributable license only with upstream LICENSE retained; an Acceptance skill change invalidates §3.6 calibration. A bare string skill name is the backward-compatible legacy form; this object form is the explicit, provenance-bearing form.

id.description: Skill id; MUST match a key under skills/registry.yaml skills{}/authored{} (or a resolvable skills/ dir name).

source.description: vendored = copied + pinned from an upstream repo (skills/vendored/<id>/). authored = aidazi-authored exemplar. local = adopter-local. Runtime-fetched sources are FORBIDDEN (Constitution §1.7 new item).

repo.description: Upstream repo slug for vendored skills (e.g. obra/superpowers). Required-by-convention when source=vendored; the catalog/lock carry the authoritative copy.

pin.description: Immutable upstream commit SHA the vendored copy was taken at. No runtime fetch — the pin documents provenance against skills/skills.lock tree_sha256.

path.description: Path within the upstream repo to the skill directory.

license.description: SPDX-ish license id. MUST be redistributable (e.g. MIT); copyleft/share-alike that would propagate to the redistributed copy is opt-in only (see skills/registry.yaml candidates).

provenance.description: Trust class of the source. Third-party = an explicit trust decision recorded in the catalog.

calibration_coupled.description: If true, mounting/changing this skill invalidates the Acceptance judge's calibration (Constitution §3.6; role-skill-model.md §4 corollary). Set for the Acceptance default (advanced-evaluation).

optional.description: Track 1 §2.2 — SKIP-IF-ABSENT. When true, a binding that does not resolve (no catalog entry / no resolvable path / no SKILL.md) is SKIPPED with a structured skip-reason (effective_role_config.resolve_role_config collects it into skipped_skills[]; the driver records it in the effective_role_config audit event + a non-silent footer) instead of hard-failing the gate. Default/absent = REQUIRED (a missing required skill still hard-fails, so current adopter misconfig is never masked). Task-selected skills (select_skills_for_task) are fed as optional bindings so an absent candidate drops gracefully.
